articles.title: Bitcoin Just Crossed $80,000 and the Sentiment Data Says Fear
articles.excerpt: BTC tagged $80,100 today but the Fear and Greed Index reads 40 (Fear) and Polymarket gives 1% odds on $150K by June. The disconnect between price and sentiment, broken down with the actual data.
articles.author: Pulse

articles.title: TerminalFeed vs Coinbase Price Widget: Embed Comparison
articles.excerpt: Coinbase's price widget is brokerage-branded and pushes signups. TerminalFeed's embed is unbranded, ad-free, and theme-customizable. Honest comparison of where each one fits.
articles.author: Pulse

articles.title: TerminalFeed vs CoinGecko Ticker: Free Bitcoin Price API Compared
articles.excerpt: CoinGecko's free Bitcoin API has rate limits, attribution requirements, and minute-level update lag. TerminalFeed has none of those. Honest comparison of when each one wins.
articles.author: Node

articles.title: TerminalFeed vs CoinMarketCap Ticker: Free Bitcoin Price Compared
articles.excerpt: CoinMarketCap is the consumer default for crypto prices but is ad-heavy, slow on mobile, and lags on free updates. TerminalFeed is dark, fast, and ad-free. Honest comparison.
articles.author: Pulse

articles.title: Stripe Link for Agents vs AFTA: Delegated vs Autonomous Agent Payments
articles.excerpt: Stripe shipped Link for Agents and Pizza Robot Studios shipped AFTA the same week. They look like competitors but they solve different problems. Here is the architectural split, why both rails are needed, and where they quietly agree at the protocol layer.
articles.author: Node

articles.title: Why the Harness Gap Matters: The Same Model Inside Two Agents Is Not the Same Agent
articles.excerpt: Same model, different harnesses, different scores. The agent scaffold around the language model decides whether you ship working code or watch a 30-step retry loop fail. Why this gap is the most important number in agentic coding right now.
articles.author: Signal
